body.title: DISCUSS: cognition delays scale with brain size — review Igor's gap in light of recent cognition work
body.result: Discussion closed (option a). Synthesis from 4 Akien refinements: cognition-delay is the SCAFFOLD other primitives plug into, not a fifth primitive. 8-question cascade with coloring-forward semantics, state-augmentation sub-cascades, persona-via-graduated-pipeline (not injected at one point), brain-size capability ceiling per cognition tier. Filed build ticket T-cognition-delay-cascade-scaffold (L) capturing the synthesis as scaffold spec; references the 4 primitive tickets that slot into it. Recency-trumps-age meta-rule already filed as palace rule. Will revise scaffold if biology research surfaces concrete delay-window structures.
body.description:
**Discussion ticket, NOT a build ticket** (per Akien 2026-04-30; one may come of it).

Source: /home/akien/TheIgorsProject/akien/ideas/DelaysForCognitionBasedOnBrainSize.txt (Kurzgesagt transcript, 'minds emerged as a gap between sensory input and motor output').

**Premise:** Across animal phylogeny, larger brains afford longer cognitive delays:
- 302-neuron roundworm: near-pure reflex, no real gap
- ~1M-neuron bee: builds + queries internal map (sun cross-reference, multi-km navigation, dance-encoding)
- 500M-neuron octopus: 40% central / 60% arm-distributed; arms make local decisions in parallel
- Billions-neuron birds (scrub jays): time-tracking on cached food, theory-of-other-minds (re-hide when watched)
- 86B-neuron human: simulate-minds-simulating-minds, fictional worlds, storytelling as social cognition transport

**The specific gap in our cognition design:**
Igor has worked on many cognition primitives (TWM, NE, milieu, reasoning_workflow, basal-ganglia habit-graph, attractor salience, BOREDOM/curiosity, anticipation primitive, planning-as-graph, etc.) — but NOT on a *bounded delay* between input and action that scales with the complexity of the situation. Currently most engram chains either fire immediately or queue up to wait their turn; there's no 'pause-and-think' window proportional to problem complexity.

**Latest-update context (why review NOW):**
- T-consult-multi-turn-follow-through (M, done) — multi-turn ConsultSession; first 'sustained internal deliberation' primitive
- T-anticipation-primitive (XL, pending) — predicted-valence-delta signal; the dopamine-like 'want before act' precursor that a delay could feed
- T-planning-as-waypoint-graph (L, pending) — graph-construction for planning; explicitly multi-step, deliberation-shaped
- T-goal-formation-from-conversation (M, pending) — goal crystallization, also delay-shaped
- pe_chain HYPOTHESIZE retry loop (just shipped today) — a tiny gap between bad-edit and re-attempt, but ad hoc, not principled

All four of these are pieces of a cognition delay that doesn't yet have a unifying primitive.

**Discussion questions:**
1. Should there be a first-class 'cognition_delay_window' primitive — a time/turn-budgeted gap that opens between stimulus and response, scaled by some complexity proxy (TWM saturation, attractor count, budget signal)?
2. What's the complexity proxy? Some candidates: (a) TWM size + arousal, (b) number of competing attractors in milieu, (c) consult-history depth, (d) reasoning_workflow depth, (e) explicit 'I'm taking a moment' marker.
3. Is the current immediate-fire-or-queue dichotomy itself the bug — i.e. we lack a 'middle gear' where cognition spins for N seconds/turns before committing?
4. How does this connect to anticipation (T-anticipation-primitive, XL)? A delay window is one place where a predicted-valence signal could be allowed to settle before action.
5. Octopus shape: do Igor's 'arm-like' subsystems (reading_pipeline, narrative_engine, milieu, COA when it lands) need to coordinate via a central pause, or run-then-coordinate?
6. Phylogeny analogy: which animal-mind level is Igor trying to be? The roundworm-to-bee transition is real and we may not have crossed it; the bee-to-bird transition (theory-of-other-minds) is a different question.

**Affected files:** N/A — discussion only. If it spawns a build ticket, likely candidates would be in cognition/ (milieu.py, reasoning_workflow.py, narrative_engine.py) or a new cognition_delay.py module.
**Design rules:** N/A discussion.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the discussion + decision on whether to build. OUT — actual implementation; a follow-up build ticket would be filed separately if we decide to act.
**Test plan:** N/A discussion. Follow-up build ticket would carry its own.

## Akien refinement (2026-05-01)

Sharper framing: **engram design and templates need to take into account how animals seem to allow a cognition cycle time to respond to thinks. A thinking time, if you will.**

This shifts the lens from "primitive in the cognition module" to "shape constraint on the engram graph itself." Practical implications to surface in the discussion:
- Engram chains may need a built-in delay-window slot (a stage the chain dwells in before firing the next link), scaled by complexity proxy
- Templates for new engram families should default to including this stage
- Phylogeny pointer: roundworm 302n (no delay), bee 1Mn (small delay), human 86Bn (long delay) — the design might want to mark which animal-mind level a given engram template corresponds to
- Connects to T-anticipation-primitive (P1, sprint-ready): a delay window is where a predicted-valence signal could settle before action commits

## Akien refinement #2 (2026-05-01) — what fills the delay

**The delay isn't empty. It's filled with internal questions.**

Why this is a ticket (not just a discussion item): we need to look at the *biology end* — what's literally happening inside that delay window? If we know, we model it. If we don't yet know, **we inject questions** — the question-cascade IS the implementation until the research lands.

### Akien's first-draft question set (from his own internal experience)

When something happens (a stimulus, a message, a sensation), an internal cascade fires:
1. **Am I safe?**
2. **Do I need to reply?**
3. **What does this mean to me?**
4. **What does the speaker mean to me?**
5. **Can I help?**
6. **What might get in the way?**
7. **Do I need to reply?** (intentionally repeated — see below)

### Why "do I need to reply?" appears twice

Humans sometimes **reply, and then reply some more.** That's the mechanism we're trying to simulate — the question re-fires *after* internal state has updated based on earlier questions in the cascade. The first firing might say "yes, but only briefly" and produce an initial reply. The second firing, after `what does this mean to me?` has settled, might say "wait, I should also mention X" and produce a follow-up turn.

So the delay isn't a single resolution; it's a question-cascade that may produce **multiple output events**, with each question potentially re-evaluating after preceding questions have shifted internal state.

### Implementation falls out (sketch — refine in the design call)

- Each question = a small probe / engram check / reasoning sub-task
- Some questions fire in parallel (am-I-safe gates faster than the rest, often resolves to 'yes' and unblocks others)
- Some questions re-fire as state evolves (the do-I-need-to-reply pattern)
- Different stimuli activate different question sets (casual greeting vs complex request)
- Question sets themselves are engram templates — adjustable per-context, per-personality

### Research thread (the biology end)

What does the literature actually say about *what fills* the cognition-delay window in animals?
- Roundworm (302n): no real delay, near-pure stimulus-response.
- Bee (1Mn): map query, sun cross-reference — these are computations during the delay; what's the structure?
- Octopus (500Mn, 40% central / 60% arm-distributed): delay involves arm-local processing. What's the question-set the arms run?
- Birds with ToM (scrub jays): theory-of-other-minds during the delay. Implies a "what does the other mind know?" question.
- Human (86Bn): simulate-minds-simulating-minds. Implies recursive question expansion.

If the biology research surfaces concrete structures (e.g., "the cascade in mammals starts with limbic-amygdala safety check, then frontal-lobe meaning, then prefrontal action-consideration"), we model that. If it doesn't, the question-cascade above IS the model — it's a reasonable working approximation drawn from human introspection.

### Connections

- **T-anticipation-primitive (P1)** — predicted-valence-delta could feed the `am I safe?` and `can I help?` questions. The delay is where anticipation gets its work done.
- **T-planning-as-waypoint-graph** — `what might get in the way?` is naturally a planning question; the delay is where the waypoint graph gets seeded.
- **T-goal-formation-from-conversation** — `what does the speaker mean to me?` and `do I need to reply?` are formation triggers.
- **engram-template framing (refinement #1):** the question-cascade is the structure. New engram families inherit a default cascade, customized per-cognition-tier.

## Akien refinement #3 (2026-05-01) — synthesis: cognition-delay as organizing primitive

The cognition-delay isn't a fifth cognition primitive sitting alongside anticipation / planning / goal-formation / consult. **It's the scaffold the others plug into.** Each is a stage within the cascade:

| Question in the cascade | Primitive that does the work |
|---|---|
| Am I safe? / Can I help? | T-anticipation-primitive (predicted-valence-delta) |
| What might get in the way? | T-planning-as-waypoint-graph (waypoint construction) |
| What does the speaker mean to me? | T-goal-formation-from-conversation (relationship-derived formation) |
| Do I need to reply? (twice) | turn-pipeline + reasoning-workflow (existing) |
| What does this mean to me? | cortex / TWM / milieu integration (existing) |

The other primitives aren't *separate machinery* — they're things that happen *during* the cognition delay window. The delay is the time and structure in which they get their work done. That's why this ticket organizes the whole next cognition push: it tells us where the others fit and how they sequence.

## Synthesis with the original "delay scales with brain size" framing

The two refinements bridge cleanly:

- **Brain size** (yesterday's framing) determines the *maximum cascade depth available*. A roundworm (302n) can run `am-I-safe` and `flee/eat` and not much more. A bee (1Mn) adds `where-am-I-relative-to-target`. A scrub jay (billions of n) adds `is-someone-watching-me-cache?`. A human (86Bn) adds the full recursive ToM stack.
- **Stimulus complexity** determines *which subset of available questions actually fires* on any given turn. A casual hello triggers a short cascade (am-I-safe → do-I-need-to-reply). A nuanced request triggers the full set with re-fires.
- **Observed delay length = cascade-actual-firing-time** = (questions fired) × (per-question time) + (re-fire passes).

So the design is: each cognition tier (matching an animal-mind level) gets a *capability ceiling* on cascade depth. Within that ceiling, the actual cascade per-turn is shaped by stimulus complexity. The "thinking time" emerges from cascade execution.

## Open questions (flag for the design call)

1. **Inhibition / cancellation.** What aborts a cascade mid-flight? E.g., a new urgent stimulus arrives, or `am-I-safe` resolves to NO and we need fight/flight not social engagement. The cascade probably wants explicit cancellation semantics + restart-with-new-cascade.
2. **Branching.** Can a question trigger a sub-cascade? E.g., `am-I-safe` = NO → spawns a threat-response cascade rather than continuing the social one. Or are these always full-cascade-restarts?
3. **Persona shaping.** Different personas run different cascade defaults. Igor's "biomimicry engineer" persona may foreground `what does this mean to me?` while a research-agent persona foregrounds `what's the next experiment?`. Where does persona inject? Is it a per-engram-template thing or a global cascade modifier?
4. **Cascade composition vs. stack.** Is the cascade strictly sequential, parallel-with-dependencies, or a mix? Akien's first-draft list reads sequential, but `am-I-safe` probably wants to fire fast and gate the rest, suggesting a dependency-graph rather than a list.
5. **Relation to existing decision_blob.** Igor already has decision-shape engrams (decision_blob, basal_ganglia, intent gate from D074). Some of the cascade questions might already exist there in different form. Migration / consolidation question for sprint.

## Akien refinement #4 (2026-05-01) — open questions resolved

### Implicit question I missed in the first-draft list

Akien's answer to (1) included a question I hadn't written down: **how do I feel about this person?** It belongs in the cascade between `what does the speaker mean to me?` and `can I help?`. Updated cascade:

1. Am I safe?
2. Do I need to reply?
3. What does this mean to me?
4. What does the speaker mean to me?
5. **How do I feel about this person?**
6. Can I help?
7. What might get in the way?
8. Do I need to reply? (re-fire after preceding answers settle)

### (1) Inhibition / cancellation — resolved as "coloring forward, not aborting"

Akien: *"if we get to how do i feel about this person, and it's negative, that's a high probability going to interfere with answering and/or alter the trajectory of the answer development process."*

The cascade doesn't get cancelled by negative answers; it gets **coloured forward**. Each question's answer enters working state and biases later questions. A negative `how do I feel about this person?` doesn't abort — it shifts the trajectory through `can I help?` and `do I need to reply?` and the eventual response shape.

### (2) Sub-cascades — resolved as "state augmentation, not recursion"

Akien: *"i don't think this kind of logic will tend to directly loop or anything. but a bad feeling about the person would lead to why i had a bad feeling about them. then look at the request along side those negative memories to decide how to reply."*

Sub-cascades aren't recursive function calls. They're **thread-following with state augmentation**: a question produces an answer that triggers a memory retrieval (or a sub-probe) which augments working state, then the cascade resumes with the enriched state. "Bad feeling → fetch why-memories → integrate → continue."

### (3) Persona shaping — resolved as "graduated through layered graph"

Akien recalls (and this matches the architecture in T-retire-legacy-direct-reasoner-path): persona isn't injected at one point. It accumulates **gradient-style through the layered cognition pipeline**:

```
words → tokens → meaning → meaning-to-me (watchlist + others) →
what could I do → what do I want to do →
how do I want to reply → how do I want to phrase → phrase → words
```

*"each layer adds more 'persona' in naturally."* Earlier layers are more universal (perception, parsing); later layers are more person-specific (voice, phrasing). The cascade questions don't inject persona — they fire AT specific layers in this graph, and persona-accumulation is already happening as the data flows through.

This maps to existing Igor architecture (per T-retire-legacy-direct-reasoner-path notes):
- thalamus.py:16 — words → tokens
- cortex.py:1920 — meanings
- basal_ganglia.py:571 (meaning_to_me layer #244) — meaning to me
- decision_blob.py + goal_formation.py — what to do
- prompt_contexts.voice_context() — how to say
- voice_ab.VoiceABFramework — put into words

The cognition-delay cascade is the *control flow* threading through these layers, not a separate persona system.

### (4) Am-I-safe shape — resolved as "fast initial categorization, override-able"

Akien: *"am i safe an amygdala thing for me, almost but not quite a substring search, certainly an initial categorization. The startle reflex makes clear that initial categorizations are not always right, so cognition does not stop there."*

So `am I safe?` is:
- **Fast pattern-match** (substring-search-like but slightly more — pattern recognition over the input)
- **Initial categorization** that gates immediate response (fight/flight/freeze if NO)
- **Override-able by later cognition** — startle reflex example: flinch first, then realize it was nothing, downgrade safety-flag, cascade continues normally
- **Not a hard final answer** — the safety flag remains evaluative throughout the cascade

Implementation: amygdala-shaped fast probe runs in parallel with the rest of the cascade. If it fires NO, it interrupts and switches to a threat-response cascade. The threat-response cascade itself is short and physical-action-oriented. Later-cognition can re-fire am-I-safe with more context and update the flag, potentially downgrading from threat-response back to social cascade.

### (5) Relation to existing decision_blob / basal_ganglia — resolved by meta-rule

Akien: *"newer descriptions hold more validity than older ones in almost all cases on this project."*

So when the cascade design (today's framing) overlaps existing decision_blob / basal_ganglia logic (older), today's framing wins as the design baseline. Migration / consolidation work at sprint-time will reshape the older code to match the cascade structure rather than the reverse.

(This meta-rule is broader than this ticket — filed as palace rule `theigors/rules/recency-trumps-age`.)
